title.raw: About — Nalla's Dad & Dog Food & Fun
title.rendered: About — Nalla’s Dad & Dog Food & Fun

content.raw:
Who Is Nalla's Dad?

I'm a software engineer in Toronto who spends an unreasonable amount of time reading ingredient labels on dog food bags. My name doesn't matter much — around here, I'm just Nalla's Dad, and that's the identity I've leaned into because it's honestly the most accurate description of my daily life. I write code for a living and obsess over kibble for a hobby. Somewhere along the way, those two things merged into this site.

How Dog Food & Fun Started

It started with frustration. Every time I searched for dog food reviews, I got one of two things: clinical articles written by people who clearly don't own dogs, or affiliate-stuffed listicles that ranked whatever paid the most as "#1 Best Pick." Neither helped me figure out what to actually feed Nalla.

So I started keeping notes. What worked, what didn't, what made her coat shine, what gave her the runs. I compared ingredient labels like I was debugging code — line by line, looking for patterns. And I realized that the information I was putting together for myself might actually be useful to other dog owners who felt the same frustration.

Dog Food & Fun is the result: honest, practical content written by someone who actually feeds a real dog every day. No sponsored rankings, no pretending to be a veterinarian, no claiming that one brand will magically fix everything. Just a person, a dog, and a lot of opinions about kibble.

The Mission

We write honest dog food reviews and practical guides for real dog owners who want to make better decisions without needing a nutrition degree. That's it. No hidden agenda, no brand partnerships dictating our opinions. When we recommend something, it's because Nalla actually ate it and I actually researched it. When something is bad, we say so — even if it's popular.

Meet Nalla

Nalla is a 4-year-old mixed breed rescue — part Lab, part something mysterious that the vet has diplomatically called "shepherd mix." She weighs about 50 pounds, has ears that can't decide if they're floppy or upright (they rotate depending on her mood), and has very strong opinions about food.

She's picky in the way that only a dog who knows she has options can be picky. She'll eat anything off the sidewalk with zero hesitation but will stare at her bowl for five minutes if the kibble brand changed. She loves eggs, tolerates broccoli, and would commit actual crimes for freeze-dried liver treats. Her favourite activities include sleeping in inconvenient locations, monitoring the neighbourhood from the window, and pretending she didn't hear "leave it."

Nalla is the unpaid food critic behind every review on this site. If she won't eat it, we don't recommend it. If she gets the runs, we mention it. Her reactions are the real-world test that no lab analysis can replace.

What You'll Find Here

Dog food reviews — honest breakdowns of specific brands and formulas, with real ingredient analysis and Nalla's actual reaction. We focus on Canadian-available brands because that's what we can actually buy and test. Practical guides — how to read labels, what ingredients actually mean, cost comparisons that help you figure out where your money goes. Written for normal people, not nutritionists. The Monthly Diary — "What Nalla Ate This Week" is our recurring series where I share exactly what went into Nalla's bowl, how she responded, and whether I'd buy it again. Think of it as a food diary, but for a dog who judges everything. Training and gear — because feeding a dog well is only half the equation. We also cover practical gear reviews and training tips from a city dog owner's perspective.

Follow Along

We're building this thing one post at a time. If you want to follow Nalla's food journey and get notified when we publish new reviews or guides, the best way is to follow us on Instagram where Nalla occasionally makes appearances (usually looking unimpressed).

Got a question about dog food, a brand you want us to review, or just want to tell us what your dog is eating? Reach out through the contact form or drop a comment on any post. We read everything.

Dog Food & Fun is an independent site. We are not veterinarians, certified animal nutritionists, or affiliated with any pet food manufacturer. We're a dog owner sharing honest experiences and research. Always consult your vet for specific dietary advice.

Nalla is a 4-year-old mixed breed rescue — part Lab, part something mysterious that the vet has diplomatically called “shepherd mix.” She weighs about 50 pounds, has ears that can’t decide if they’re floppy or upright (they rotate depending on her mood), and has very strong opinions about food.

She’s picky in the way that only a dog who knows she has options can be picky. She’ll eat anything off the sidewalk with zero hesitation but will stare at her bowl for five minutes if the kibble brand changed. She loves eggs, tolerates broccoli, and would commit actual crimes for freeze-dried liver treats. Her favourite activities include sleeping in inconvenient locations, monitoring the neighbourhood from the window, and pretending she didn’t hear “leave it.”

Nalla is the unpaid food critic behind every review on this site. If she won’t eat it, we don’t recommend it. If she gets the runs, we mention it. Her reactions are the real-world test that no lab analysis can replace.

What You’ll Find Here

Dog food reviews — honest breakdowns of specific brands and formulas, with real ingredient analysis and Nalla’s actual reaction. We focus on Canadian-available brands because that’s what we can actually buy and test.

Practical guides — how to read labels, what ingredients actually mean, cost comparisons that help you figure out where your money goes. Written for normal people, not nutritionists.

The Monthly Diary — “What Nalla Ate This Week” is our recurring series where I share exactly what went into Nalla’s bowl, how she responded, and whether I’d buy it again. Think of it as a food diary, but for a dog who judges everything.

Training and gear — because feeding a dog well is only half the equation. We also cover practical gear reviews and training tips from a city dog owner’s perspective.

Follow Along

We’re building this thing one post at a time. If you want to follow Nalla’s food journey and get notified when we publish new reviews or guides, the best way is to follow us on Instagram where Nalla occasionally makes appearances (usually looking unimpressed).

Got a question about dog food, a brand you want us to review, or just want to tell us what your dog is eating? Reach out through the contact form or drop a comment on any post. We read everything.

Dog Food & Fun is an independent site. We are not veterinarians, certified animal nutritionists, or affiliated with any pet food manufacturer. We’re a dog owner sharing honest experiences and research. Always consult your vet for specific dietary advice.

No hype. Just data. And Nalla.

50+ Brands ReviewedIndependent ReviewsNalla-Approved

Join Nalla and me as we dig into honest product reviews, nutritional facts, and tips to keep your best friend happy and healthy.

Explore top picks

Hi, I'm Nalla's Dad

Engineer by Day, Dog Nutrition Researcher by Night

I’m not a veterinarian. I’m a software engineer who got frustrated by vague pet food marketing and applied the same analytical mindset I use at work — reading ingredient labels like spec sheets, comparing cost-per-serving like infrastructure costs, and refusing to accept “premium” as a data point.

This site is what I wish existed when I got Nalla: honest, numbers-driven breakdowns without the fluff.

01

Grooming

Browse →
Simple DIY guides to keep your dog’s coat healthy and clean without the stress of a salon visit.

02

Food & Diet

Browse →
We decode the labels. Honest breakdowns of kibble, wet food, and treats to help you avoid the marketing hype.

03

Lifestyle & Gear

Browse →
From durable toys to comfortable beds—gear reviews tested by Nalla herself for everyday living.

04

Training

Browse →
Positive reinforcement tips and practical tricks to strengthen the bond between you and your dog.

Spring Shedding Survival Guide: I Tested 5 De-Shedding Tools on Nalla (Here’s the Data)

Data-Driven Emergency Dog Recall Training: Whistle Protocol

Read More

Engineer’s Data-Driven Guide: Canned Pumpkin for Dogs

Read More

Engineer’s Guide: Teach Dog Place Command for Remote…

Read More

Join Our Community of Analytical Dog Owners

How We Review — Our 3-Step Process

01

Ingredient Forensics

We parse every AAFCO label — named proteins, fillers, controversial additives, and hidden carbohydrate loads. No marketing copy accepted.

02

Comparative Benchmarking

Every product is measured against 2–3 direct competitors on cost-per-serving, protein density, and ingredient quality score.

03

Real-World Durability Testing

Where applicable, we test gear on Nalla and document results over weeks — not a single afternoon.

content.raw:
Contact Our Dog Nutrition Experts

Navigating the world of canine nutrition can be complex. We're passionate about helping you provide the best diet for your dog's unique needs. Whether you have a question about specific ingredients, need advice on transitioning to a new food, or just want some fun, healthy treat ideas, our team is here to help.

Send Us Your Question

Please fill out the form below, and we'll get back to you as soon as we can. We typically respond within 24-48 business hours.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I choose the right food for my dog?

Look for a food that is labeled "complete and balanced" by AAFCO standards. Consider your dog's age (puppy, adult, senior), breed size, and activity level. When in doubt, ingredients like a named meat source (e.g., chicken, beef) as the first ingredient are a good sign.

What are common signs of a nutritional deficiency in dogs?

Signs can be subtle but may include a dull, dry coat, flaky skin, low energy levels, digestive issues, or changes in stool. If you notice any of these, it's a good idea to assess their diet and consult a professional.

When should I talk to a vet about my dog's diet?

Always consult your veterinarian before making significant dietary changes, especially if your dog has health issues like allergies, kidney disease, or is overweight. They can provide tailored advice based on your dog's specific medical history and needs.
content.rendered:
Get in Touch — Dog Nutrition Questions Welcome

Have a question about an ingredient, a product recommendation, or just want to share what’s working for your dog? I read every message personally. As an engineer, I try to give you specific, actionable answers — not generic advice.

Whether you’re switching your dog’s food, dealing with a picky eater, or just curious about a product I’ve reviewed — send it over. No question is too basic.

Contact Us:

Have questions about nutrition or need fun ideas for your pup? We’d love to hear from you! Fill out the form below.

Subscription Form

Got a question about dog food, ingredients, or what to actually feed your pup? You’ve come to the right place. I’m Gil — a software engineer and Nalla’s dad. I spend way too much time reading ingredient panels and researching what actually goes into dog food. Not a vet, not a nutritionist — just a dog owner who did the homework so you don’t have to. Drop me a message and I’ll share what I know.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I know if I’m feeding my dog the right amount?

Feeding guidelines on dog food bags are a great starting point, but the ideal amount depends on your dog’s age, breed, weight, and activity level. For a personalized recommendation, monitor your dog’s body condition and consult with your vet or one of our nutrition experts.

Is a grain-free diet better for my dog?

Not necessarily. While some dogs have specific grain sensitivities, most dogs digest grains well, and they can be a valuable source of nutrients. The best diet is one that is complete, balanced, and appropriate for your individual dog’s health needs. We recommend discussing any major dietary changes with a professional.

What are the signs of a food allergy in dogs?

Common signs of food allergies or sensitivities include itchy skin, chronic ear infections, paw licking, and gastrointestinal issues like vomiting or diarrhea. If you suspect an allergy, it’s crucial to work with your veterinarian to identify the trigger through a proper elimination diet.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I know if I'm feeding my dog the right amount?

Feeding guidelines on dog food bags are a great starting point, but the ideal amount depends on your dog's age, breed, weight, and activity level. For a personalized recommendation, monitor your dog's body condition and consult with your vet or one of our nutrition experts.

Is a grain-free diet better for my dog?

Not necessarily. While some dogs have specific grain sensitivities, most dogs digest grains well, and they can be a valuable source of nutrients. The best diet is one that is complete, balanced, and appropriate for your individual dog's health needs. We recommend discussing any major dietary changes with a professional.

What are the signs of a food allergy in dogs?

Common signs of food allergies or sensitivities include itchy skin, chronic ear infections, paw licking, and gastrointestinal issues like vomiting or diarrhea. If you suspect an allergy, it's crucial to work with your veterinarian to identify the trigger through a proper elimination diet.

Privacy Policy

Last updated: March 2026

Welcome to Dog Food & Fun (dogfoodandfun.com). This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information when you visit our site.

Affiliate Disclosure

This site participates in affiliate marketing programs. When you click on certain product links and make a purchase, we may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you. These commissions help keep the site running and allow us to continue producing honest, research-based content. We only recommend products we have personally researched or tested.

Information We Collect

We may collect the following types of information:

  • Analytics data: We use Google Analytics (or similar tools) to understand how visitors use our site. This includes pages visited, time spent, device type, and general geographic location. This data is anonymous and aggregated.
  • Email newsletter data: If you subscribe to our newsletter, we collect your email address. We use this only to send dog nutrition tips and site updates. We never sell or share your email with third parties.
  • Contact form data: If you contact us via our contact page, we receive your name, email, and message.

Cookies

This site uses cookies to improve your browsing experience. Cookies may be set by:

  • Google Analytics (for traffic analysis)
  • Affiliate networks (to track referral clicks)
  • WordPress (for site functionality)

You can disable cookies in your browser settings at any time, though some site features may not function properly as a result.

Affiliate Links

Some links on this site are affiliate links. This means we may earn a commission if you purchase a product after clicking through our link. We are required by the U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and other regulatory bodies to disclose this relationship. Our editorial opinions are never influenced by affiliate relationships — we only recommend products we believe in.

Third-Party Links

This site contains links to third-party websites (such as product pages on Amazon, Chewy, or other retailers). We are not responsible for the privacy practices or content of those sites. We encourage you to review the privacy policy of any third-party site you visit.

GDPR — EU Visitors

This site is operated from Israel. If you are visiting from the European Union, you have rights under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), including the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. To exercise these rights, contact us via the Contact page.

Data Retention

Newsletter subscriber data is retained until you unsubscribe. Analytics data is retained per the default settings of the analytics provider (typically 26 months). Contact form submissions are retained for 12 months.

Children's Privacy

This site is not directed at children under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.

Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated date. Continued use of the site after any changes constitutes your acceptance of the updated policy.

Contact

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please reach out via our Contact page.

content.raw:
Smart Nutrition for Happy Dogs

"I believe every dog deserves to thrive. I combine a data-driven approach with a love for dogs to cut through the marketing hype and find the best nutrition for your furry best friend."

THE ORIGIN STORY

I Realized Most Dog Food Labels Were Confusing or Misleading. I Wasn't Satisfied with 'Good Enough' for My Dog, So I Decided to Do the Research Myself.

I created Dog Food & Fun to be the resource I wish I had. I research ingredients, analyze nutritional profiles, and break down complex health info into simple, actionable advice. My goal is to help you make confident decisions so your dog can live a longer, healthier, and happier life.

It all started when I tried to find the perfect food for my own dog. I was overwhelmed by the vague labels, confusing ingredients, and flashy marketing that didn't tell the whole story. As an engineer, I’m used to solving complex problems with logic and data—so I decided to apply that same mindset to dog nutrition.
content.rendered:
Engineer. Dog Owner. Skeptic of Marketing Hype.

I’m an engineer by trade and a dog owner by choice. When my dog Nalla was a puppy, I started researching dog nutrition the same way I approach any engineering problem: with data, skepticism, and a refusal to accept marketing claims at face value.

What I found surprised me. Most dog food labels are designed to confuse. Most “expert” advice online is vague, sponsored, or both. So I started writing down what I actually learned — ingredient by ingredient, cost per serving, real-world testing. That became this site.

THE ORIGIN STORY

I Realized Most Dog Food Labels Were Confusing or Misleading. I Wasn’t Satisfied with ‘Good Enough’ for My Dog, So I Decided to Do the Research Myself.

I created Dog Food & Fun to be the resource I wish I had. I research ingredients, analyze nutritional profiles, and break down complex health info into simple, actionable advice. My goal is to help you make confident decisions so your dog can live a longer, healthier, and happier life.

It all started when I tried to find the perfect food for my own dog. I was overwhelmed by the vague labels, confusing ingredients, and flashy marketing that didn’t tell the whole story. As an engineer, I’m used to solving complex problems with logic and data—so I decided to apply that same mindset to dog nutrition.

My Methodology

Every review and recommendation on this site follows the same engineering framework:

  • Ingredient analysis: I read the full AAFCO label, not just the marketing copy. I check for named protein sources, identify fillers, and flag potential allergens or controversial additives.
  • Cost-per-serving math: “Premium” kibble is sometimes cheaper than budget brands per calorie. I do the math so you don’t have to.
  • Comparison testing: When I review a product, I compare it against at least 2-3 competitors on objective criteria — not just “my dog liked it.”
  • Sourcing: I prioritize peer-reviewed veterinary nutrition research, AAFCO guidelines, and real-world feeding trials over influencer opinions.

What I Am — and What I’m Not

✅ What I am:
An engineer who has spent years researching dog nutrition for Nalla. Someone who reads the actual studies, not the press releases.		 ❌ What I’m not:
A veterinarian. A pet nutritionist. A brand partner. Nothing on this site is medical advice — always consult a licensed vet for health decisions.

About Nalla

Nalla is a Labrador Retriever mix living in Tel Aviv. She’s the reason this site exists. She has a sensitive stomach, loves to run, and has absolutely no patience for dogs who don’t want to play. She has tried — and given honest feedback on — more dog foods than I care to admit.

Transparency

When I recommend products, this site sometimes earns a small commission through affiliate links. It never changes my recommendation. I have turned down sponsored partnerships because I didn’t believe in the product. My test: would I give this to Nalla?

You can read the full Privacy Policy and Affiliate Disclosure for details.

Questions? Reach out via the Contact page — I read every message.

My Methodology

Every review and recommendation on this site follows the same engineering framework:

  • Ingredient analysis: I read the full AAFCO label, not just the marketing copy. I check for named protein sources, identify fillers, and flag potential allergens or controversial additives.
  • Cost-per-serving math: "Premium" kibble is sometimes cheaper than budget brands per calorie. I do the math so you don't have to.
  • Comparison testing: When I review a product, I compare it against at least 2-3 competitors on objective criteria — not just "my dog liked it."
  • Sourcing: I prioritize peer-reviewed veterinary nutrition research, AAFCO guidelines, and real-world feeding trials over influencer opinions.

About Nalla

Nalla is a Labrador Retriever mix living in Tel Aviv. She's the reason this site exists. She has a sensitive stomach, loves to run, and has absolutely no patience for dogs who don't want to play. She has tried — and given honest feedback on — more dog foods than I care to admit.

Transparency

When I recommend products, this site sometimes earns a small commission through affiliate links. It never changes my recommendation. I have turned down sponsored partnerships because I didn't believe in the product. My test: would I give this to Nalla?

You can read the full Privacy Policy and Affiliate Disclosure for details.

Questions? Reach out via the Contact page — I read every message.
